          - paragraph [ref=e148]:
            - text: Anders Hejlsberg (
            - link /ˈhaɪlzbɜːrɡ/ [ref=e151] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Help:IPA/English
              - text: /
              - generic [ref=e152]: ˈhaɪlzbɜːrɡ
              - text: /
            - text: "; Danish:"
            - link "[ˈhɑjlsˌpɛɐ̯ˀ]" [ref=e154] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Help:IPA/Danish
            - text: ; born 2 December 1960)
            - superscript [ref=e155]:
              - link "[2]" [ref=e156] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#cite_note-SH3-2"
            - text: is a
            - link "Danish" [ref=e157] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Denmark
            - link "software engineer" [ref=e158] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Software_engineer
            - text: who co-designed several
            - link "programming languages" [ref=e159] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Programming_language
            - text: and development tools. He was the original author of
            - link "Turbo Pascal" [ref=e160] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Turbo_Pascal
            - text: and the chief architect of
            - link "Delphi" [ref=e161] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Delphi_(programming_language)
            - text: . He currently works for
            - link "Microsoft" [ref=e162] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Microsoft
            - text: as the lead architect of
            - link "C#" [ref=e163] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/C_Sharp_(programming_language)
            - superscript [ref=e164]:
              - link "[1]" [ref=e165] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#cite_note-MTF-1"
            - text: and core developer on
            - link "TypeScript" [ref=e166] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/TypeScript
            - text: .
            - superscript [ref=e167]:
              - link "[3]" [ref=e168] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#cite_note-Typescript-3"
          - generic [ref=e169]:
            - heading "Early life" [level=2] [ref=e170]
            - generic [ref=e171]:
              - text: "["
              - link "edit" [ref=e172] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: /w/index.php?title=Anders_Hejlsberg&action=edit&section=1
              - text: "]"
          - paragraph [ref=e173]:
            - text: Hejlsberg was born in
            - link "Copenhagen" [ref=e174] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Copenhagen
            - text: ","
            - link "Denmark" [ref=e175] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Denmark
            - text: ","
            - superscript [ref=e176]:
              - link "[4]" [ref=e177] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#cite_note-4"
            - text: and studied Electrical Engineering at the
            - link "Technical University of Denmark" [ref=e178] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Technical_University_of_Denmark
            - text: . While at the university in 1980, he began writing programs for the
            - link "Nascom" [ref=e179] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Nascom
            - link "microcomputer" [ref=e180] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Microcomputer
            - text: ", including a"
            - link "Pascal" [ref=e181] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Pascal_(programming_language)
            - text: compiler which was initially marketed as the Blue Label Software Pascal for the
            - link "Nascom-2" [ref=e182] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Nascom-2
            - text: . However, he soon rewrote it for
            - link "CP/M" [ref=e183] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/CP/M
            - text: and
            - link "DOS" [ref=e184] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/DOS
            - text: ", marketing it first as Compas Pascal and later as PolyPascal. Later the product was licensed to"
            - link "Borland" [ref=e185] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Borland
            - text: ", and integrated into an"
            - link "IDE" [ref=e186] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Integrated_development_environment
            - text: to become the
            - link "Turbo Pascal" [ref=e187] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Turbo_Pascal
            - text: system. Turbo Pascal competed with PolyPascal. The compiler itself was largely inspired by the "Tiny Pascal" compiler in
            - link "Niklaus Wirth" [ref=e188] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Niklaus_Wirth
            - text: "'s"
            - link "Algorithms + Data Structures = Programs" [ref=e190] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Algorithms_%2B_Data_Structures_%3D_Programs
            - text: ", one of the most influential computer science books of the time."
            - superscript [ref=e191]:
              - link "[5]" [ref=e192] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#cite_note-ACM-5"
          - generic [ref=e193]:
            - heading "At Borland" [level=2] [ref=e194]
            - generic [ref=e195]:
              - text: "["
              - link "edit" [ref=e196] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: /w/index.php?title=Anders_Hejlsberg&action=edit&section=2
              - text: "]"
          - paragraph [ref=e197]:
            - text: In Borland's hands, Turbo Pascal became one of the most commercially successful Pascal compilers.
            - superscript [ref=e198]:
              - link "[6]" [ref=e199] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#cite_note-Pascal-6"
            - text: Hejlsberg remained with PolyData until the company came under financial stress and in 1989 he moved to California to become Chief Engineer at Borland. During this time, he developed Turbo Pascal further and became the chief architect for the team that produced
            - link "Borland Delphi" [ref=e200] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Borland_Delphi
            - text: ", which replaced Turbo Pascal."
            - superscript [ref=e201]:
              - link "[7]" [ref=e202] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#cite_note-7"
          - generic [ref=e203]:
            - heading "At Microsoft" [level=2] [ref=e204]
            - generic [ref=e205]:
              - text: "["
              - link "edit" [ref=e206] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: /w/index.php?title=Anders_Hejlsberg&action=edit&section=3
              - text: "]"
          - figure "Hejlsberg at the Professional Developers Conference 2008" [ref=e207]:
            - link [ref=e208] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/File:Anders_Hejlsberg_at_PDC2008.jpg
              - img [ref=e209]
            - generic [ref=e210]: Hejlsberg at the Professional Developers Conference 2008
          - paragraph [ref=e211]:
            - text: In 1996, Hejlsberg left Borland and joined
            - link "Microsoft" [ref=e212] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Microsoft
            - text: . One of his first achievements was the
            - link "J++" [ref=e213] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Visual_J%2B%2B
            - text: programming language and the
            - link "Windows Foundation Classes" [ref=e214] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Visual_J%2B%2B#The_WFC
            - text: ; he also became a Microsoft Distinguished Engineer and
            - link "Technical Fellow" [ref=e215] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Fellow#Industry_and_corporate_fellowships
            - text: . Since 2000, he has been the lead architect of the team developing the
            - link "C#" [ref=e216] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/C_Sharp_(programming_language)
            - text: language. In 2012 Hejlsberg announced a new Microsoft project,
            - link "TypeScript" [ref=e217] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/TypeScript
            - text: ", a superset of"
            - link "JavaScript" [ref=e218] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/JavaScript
            - text: .
            - superscript [ref=e219]:
              - link "[8]" [ref=e220] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#cite_note-8"
            - superscript [ref=e221]:
              - link "[9]" [ref=e222] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#cite_note-9"
          - generic [ref=e223]:
            - heading "Awards" [level=2] [ref=e224]
            - generic [ref=e225]:
              - text: "["
              - link "edit" [ref=e226] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: /w/index.php?title=Anders_Hejlsberg&action=edit&section=4
              - text: "]"
          - paragraph [ref=e227]:
            - text: Hejlsberg received the 2001
            - link "Dr. Dobb's Excellence in Programming Award" [ref=e228] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Dr._Dobb%27s_Excellence_in_Programming_Award
            - text: for his work on Turbo Pascal, Delphi, C# and the Microsoft
            - link ".NET Framework" [ref=e229] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/.NET_Framework
            - text: .
            - superscript [ref=e230]:
              - link "[10]" [ref=e231] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#cite_note-10"
          - paragraph [ref=e232]:
            - text: Together with
            - link "Shon Katzenberger" [ref=e233] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /w/index.php?title=Shon_Katzenberger&action=edit&redlink=1
            - text: ","
            - link "Scott Wiltamuth" [ref=e234] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /w/index.php?title=Scott_Wiltamuth&action=edit&redlink=1
            - text: ","
            - link "Todd Proebsting" [ref=e235] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /w/index.php?title=Todd_Proebsting&action=edit&redlink=1
            - text: ","
            - link "Erik Meijer" [ref=e236] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /wiki/Erik_Meijer_(computer_scientist)
            - text: ","
            - link "Peter Hallam" [ref=e237] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /w/index.php?title=Peter_Hallam&action=edit&redlink=1
            - text: ", and"
            - link "Peter Sollich" [ref=e238] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /w/index.php?title=Peter_Sollich&action=edit&redlink=1
            - text: ", Anders was awarded a Technical Recognition Award for Outstanding Technical Achievement for their work on the C# language in 2007."
            - superscript [ref=e239]:
              - link "[11]" [ref=e240] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#cite_note-Channel9-11"
